user_message: This just came in from Thomas Okafor at Azure Ridge Partners. Can you pull the context?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Thomas Okafor <thomas.okafor@azureridgepa.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-28 12:13 EST
Subject: Follow-up: renewal risk Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about renewal risk. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Thomas Okafor <thomas.okafor@azureridgepa.com>
Date: 2024-01-18 14:32 EST
Subject: Follow-up: renewal risk Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about renewal risk. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: renewal risk Discussion - Azure Ridge Partners
Date: 2024-01-05
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners), Raj Mehta (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is renewal risk.

[00:06:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:10:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

[00:12:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:16:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on technical validation and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:19:00] Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Pre-Meeting Brief template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct renewal risk decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
